issues.body: routing_labels returns `(effective | verdict["add"]) - base` but discards `verdict["remove"]`. If a future checked-in template supplies `status:untriaged`, a conflicting `role:*`, or another label triage is designed to replace, evaluate() will append additions without removing the obsolete label and can mint an ambiguous issue. Current templates are unaffected, and the new all-template test would catch role/status ambiguity when such a template is added, so this is not blocking this PR.
issues.fix_hint: Have routing_labels return the complete final routing-label set or apply both add and remove deltas when constructing labels_full, e.g. compute `(effective | verdict["add"]) - verdict["remove"]`, then derive the labels to append/remove from the original list. Add a focused test with a conflicting preexisting role or status label.
issues.title: routing_labels ignores labels triage requests removing

summary: The change correctly routes newly minted flow-on issues at creation and safely extends retriage to the pre-fix status-less backlog. The focused flow-on and retriage self-tests pass and exercise role derivation, default priority, ready status, restricted github-actions[bot] trust, and no churn for already-routed issues. One non-blocking robustness gap remains: routing_labels applies triage additions but not removals, though the checked-in rule templates do not currently contain conflicting routing labels and the exhaustive template test protects that invariant.
